text:
Three separate run folders — each `-c -p` command started a new proxy session because each `claude` invocation is a separate process. The proxy creates a new run folder per startup. But the Claude sessions do continue via `-c`.

Let me wait for turn 3 to finish:

tool_uses.name: Bash
tool_uses.input: {"command": "RUN_DIR=/tmp/claude_captures/run_20260407_191222 && sleep 60 && PREV=$(ls \"$RUN_DIR\" | grep \"_summary.txt$\" | grep -v response | wc -l) && sleep 30 && CURR=$(ls \"$RUN_DIR\" | grep \"_summary.txt$\" | grep -v response | wc -l) && echo \"Was $PREV, now $CURR — $([ $PREV -eq $CURR ] && echo 'STABLE' || echo 'still running')\"", "description": "Wait for turn 3 in latest folder", "timeout": 120000}
